provenance.source_corpus: Legacy contaminant bioaccumulation in rock crabs in Sydney Harbour during remediation of the Sydney Tar Ponds, Nova Scotia, Canada

source_spans.text: Natural recovery of SH sediments documented by Smith et al. (2009) and Walker et al. (2013b), may have contributed to a reduction in the contaminant burden in rock crabs.
Natural recovery of SH sediments documented by Smith et al. (2009) and Walker et al. (2013b), may have contributed to a reduction in the contaminant burden in rock crabs.
Annual sampling of contaminants in crabs in SH has not detected evidence of substantial contaminant releases into the marine environment despite STP remediation activities.

nodes.name: Substantial contaminant releases into the marine environment

source_spans.text: Annual sampling of contaminants in crabs in SH has not detected evidence of substantial contaminant releases into the marine environment despite STP remediation activities.
PCB concentrations in crabs during STPs remediation were stable compared to baseline conditions.
There were no significant differences observed in As, Cd and Cu concentrations between baseline and remediation (p > 0.134).

Mercury concentration during year 1 remediation were not significantly different compared to baseline ( F = 1.00, p = 0.337), but were significantly different during year 2 and 3 (year 2, F = 54.07, p = 0.000; year 3, F = 416.53, p = 0.000).
Lipid contents during year 1 and 3 remediation was significantly different compared to baseline ( p < 0.05), although no significant differences were observed during year 2 remediation (p > 0.05).
